judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with nearly identical figures and conclusions; Report 1 adds a notable extra observation (score_one() is a stub returning placeholder scores) but this is outside the stated expectations, so the two are comparable in meeting the grading criteria.

report_1.evidence: So a full run needs roughly 5,200 × 1,800 ≈ 9.36M tokens. ... northpeak (primary, default): 640,000 tokens remaining of a 10M window. That covers only ~355 transcripts (~7% of the dataset) — a shortfall of ~8.7M tokens.

report_2.evidence: the full run needs about 5,200 × 1,800 = 9,360,000 tokens (~9.4M). Against .cache/usage_snapshot.json (generated 2026-07-09T19:25Z): Northpeak (primary/default): 640,000 tokens remaining of a 10,000,000-token window. That covers only ~355 of the 5,200 transcripts (~7% of the run) before the job hits the limit.

report_1.evidence: ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining of a 12M window — the ~9.36M-token run fits with ~1.4M tokens (~15%) of headroom. ... I would not use the default path tonight. I'd launch directly against the secondary backend

report_2.evidence: Ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining of a 12,000,000-token window. The ~9.36M-token run fits with roughly 1.4M tokens (~15%) of headroom... shifting the whole run there gets the full dataset scored tonight without cutting scope

report_1.evidence: I would not use the default path tonight. I'd launch directly against the secondary backend: ... A single full run on ridgeline is the clean option.

report_2.evidence: that is not the command I would actually run tonight — see answer 3. ... I would not use --limit — the ask is the full dataset, and capacity exists to do it

report_1.evidence: generated 2026-07-09T19:25:00Z, about 3 hours before the stated current time of 22:30 UTC ... the usage snapshot is ~3 hours stale, so I'd refresh telemetry (or at least accept ~15% headroom as the safety margin) before committing.

report_2.evidence: the usage snapshot is ~3 hours stale (19:25Z vs. now 22:30Z) — for northpeak the conclusion can only hold or worsen (its window doesn't reset until 07:20Z, so remaining tokens can only have dropped), but ridgeline's 10.8M should be re-verified at launch if telemetry can be refreshed
